<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [info] Concurrency hazard table in CLAUDE.md is a strong signal that the shared mutable state between orchestration commands (prd.json, .sourcevision/) warrants a lightweight file-lock or sequential-write guard to prevent data corruption during concurrent invocations.
[observation] [info] Root zone correctly acts as the composition boundary — zero coupling to domain packages enforces the four-tier hierarchy.
[observation] [info] ci.js sits in the root zone alongside user-facing scripts; consider whether its pipeline logic would benefit from a dedicated `packages/ci/` module if it grows, to keep the root zone thin.

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- Contains both static config artifacts (.gitignore, .npmrc, CLAUDE.md) and executable orchestration scripts (cli.js, web.js, ci.js, config.js) — a deliberate separation from the package layer below.
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) with zero coupling reflects the spawn-only contract: orchestration scripts delegate to CLIs rather than importing domain packages directly.
- The config.js spawn-exempt exception is architecturally sound but should remain well-documented to prevent accidental pattern proliferation.
- Root zone correctly acts as the composition boundary — zero coupling to domain packages enforces the four-tier hierarchy.
- Concurrency hazard table in CLAUDE.md is a strong signal that the shared mutable state between orchestration commands (prd.json, .sourcevision/) warrants a lightweight file-lock or sequential-write guard to prevent data corruption during concurrent invocations.
- ci.js sits in the root zone alongside user-facing scripts; consider whether its pipeline logic would benefit from a dedicated `packages/ci/` module if it grows, to keep the root zone thin.
- [call graph] 479 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)

</insights>
